location.office_time_required: at least 3 days per week, including Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

level_signals: 4+ years leading enterprise technology program delivery
End-to-end program ownership across corporate engineering, IT, privacy, security

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4+ years leading enterprise technology program delivery, with demonstrated experience in IT infrastructure and operations (network engineering, systems engineering, identity management, endpoint/MDM management, SaaS and collaboration platform migrations, and/or IT service management)
Experience executing full-cycle technology transformations from design through implementation, change management, and operational handoff
Strong executive communication skills with ability to support executive level communications and translate complex technical and regulatory requirements into business context
Demonstrated experience managing technology vendors, and experience tracking and reporting on program budgets in partnership with finance stakeholders
Track record coordinating cross-functional teams across IT, Engineering, Security, Privacy, Finance, People, and data operations
Ability to understand strategic goals and translate them into tactical execution plans

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Ideal candidate also has experience supporting one of the following: M&A technology integrations, HCM platforms (Workday, Greenhouse), ERP implementations (Oracle Financials, Anaplan), CRM rollouts (Salesforce), security tool deployments, or privacy compliance initiatives
Support AI and automation initiatives by identifying opportunities to leverage AI for process optimization and continuous improvement
Business acumen with the ability to support planning discussions and contribute recommendations that align with key business partner needs

scope.surface: Corporate Engineering, IT, Privacy and Security technology initiatives
primary_metrics_inferred: Program delivery on time and on budget
core_responsibilities: Execute end-to-end program delivery for technology initiatives such as IT infrastructure transformations, financial technology transformations, people technology transformations, sales technology rollouts, privacy compliance programs, and security implementations
Support and contribute to strategic planning sessions and cross-functional prioritization with Corporate Engineering, IT, Privacy and Security leadership teams
Manage program governance through biweekly and monthly strategic initiative reviews, risk assessments, and executive reporting
Coordinate cross-functional program teams spanning engineering, IT operations, security, privacy, compliance, and business stakeholders
Support enterprise technology lifecycle management including vendor selection, contract negotiations, system integrations, and sunset/migration planning
Partner with finance and strategy teams to manage program budgets and track ROI

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned but not detailed

green_flags: Hybrid schedule clearly defined (3 days/week: Monday, Wednesday, Thursday)
Broad and well-defined program scope across Corporate Engineering, IT, Privacy, and Security
Explicit mention that Lyft employees make ultimate hiring decisions despite AI screening use
Fills an existing vacancy — active headcount
Strong benefits package including parental leave, RRSP match, flexible PTO
red_flags: Compensation stated in CAD only — role is Toronto-based, limiting applicant pool to Canada
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned at all
Broad scope across IT infrastructure, ERP, HCM, CRM, privacy, and security may indicate under-resourced program function
Mandatory in-office days (Mon/Wed/Thu) reduces flexibility for hybrid candidates
